title: Data Types
description: Explore the fundamental data types in Swift: String, Int, Double, Boolean, and more.

sections.content:
## Data Types in Swift

Every value in Swift has a type. Understanding types helps you write correct code.

### Common Types

| Type | Description | Example |
|------|-------------|---------|
| `String` | Text | `"Hello"` |
| `Int` | Whole numbers | `42` |
| `Double` | Decimal numbers | `3.14` |
| `Bool` | True or false | `true` |
| `Character` | Single character | `"A"` |

### Type Safety

Swift is type-safe - it checks types at compile time:

```swift
var name: String = "Alice"  // Explicit type
var age: Int = 25           // Explicit type
var price: Double = 19.99   // Explicit type
var isActive: Bool = true   // Explicit type
```

### Type Inference

Swift can infer types from initial values:

```swift
var name = "Alice"  // Inferred as String
var age = 25        // Inferred as Int
```

sections.title: Working with Types
sections.starterCode:
// Explicitly typed variables
let city: String = "San Francisco"
let population: Int = 884363
let area: Double = 121.4
let isCapital: Bool = false

print("\(city) has a population of \(population)")
print("Area: \(area) sq km")
print("Is capital: \(isCapital)")
sections.expectedOutput:
San Francisco has a population of 884363
Area: 121.4 sq km
Is capital: false
sections.solution:
let city: String = "San Francisco"
let population: Int = 884363
let area: Double = 121.4
let isCapital: Bool = false
print("\(city) has a population of \(population)")
print("Area: \(area) sq km")
print("Is capital: \(isCapital)")

sections.title: Type Conversion
sections.instructions: Create an Int variable for your age and a Double for your height, then print both.

sections.question: What type would Swift infer for the value 3.14?
sections.explanation: Swift infers decimal numbers as Double by default, not Float.
